code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "its runway is named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "ICAO code is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "location identifier is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "IATA code is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the first runway is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the first runway is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "the first runway surface is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "the runway surface is {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "its runway is named {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {obj}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {obj}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "has leader party {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "chief": "has chief {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "has native name {obj}",
        "class": "class is {obj}",
        "division": "division is {obj}",
        "order": "order is {obj}",
        "species": "species is {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym is {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        # If there is exactly one triple and it is a simple quantitative predicate,
        # produce a more natural standalone sentence.
        if len(tlist) == 1:
            t = tlist[0]
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            if t.predicate == "runwayLength":
                sentence = f"The runway length of {subject} is {obj} metres."
                sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
            elif t.predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
                sentence = f"The elevation of {subject} is {obj} metres above sea level."
                sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
            elif t.predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
                sentence = f"The elevation of {subject} is {obj} feet above sea level."
                sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
            elif t.predicate == "cityServed":
                sentence = f"{subject} serves the city of {obj}."
                sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
        # General case: build clause list
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
